books.title: Titus Andronicus
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: A blood feud erupts between a famous Roman general and the Queen of the Goths.

books.title: Captain Blood
books.author: Rafael Sabatini
books.summary: An English doctor is caught up in a rebellion and sentenced to transportation to the Caribbean, but later escapes slavery to become a pirate.

books.title: Our Nig
books.author: Harriet E. Wilson
books.summary: An autobiographical account of a young biracial girl abandoned by her parents and forced into servitude in the northern United States.

books.title: Eminent Victorians
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: Four witty and biting biographies of Victorians that take aim at the moral superiority of their subject’s time.

books.title: Jibby Jones
books.author: Ellis Parker Butler
books.summary: Five boys who live near the Mississippi River embark on an adventure to find hidden pirate treasure.

books.title: Love Among the Chickens
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: Stanley Ukridge ensnares the narrator in another one of his schemes: running a chicken farm in the English countryside.

books.title: King Lear
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: An aging king divides his kingdom between his two eldest daughters, slighting the third, and comes to bitterly regret his decision.

books.title: The Well at the World’s End
books.author: William Morris
books.summary: A king’s son embarks on an adventure to find a legendary well that grants long life.

books.title: Mr. Standfast
books.author: John Buchan
books.summary: An army general chases a network of German spies through Scotland, the Swiss Alps, and the First World War’s Western Front.

books.title: The House on the Borderland
books.author: William Hope Hodgson
books.summary: An account of the terrifying metaphysical haunting of a recluse’s secluded home.

books.title: Red Dusk and the Morrow
books.author: Paul Dukes
books.summary: A newly-minted secret agent is sent to pick up the pieces of Britain’s spy networks after they are shattered by the Red Terror.

books.title: The Box Office Murders
books.author: Freeman Wills Crofts
books.summary: The discovery of a drowned woman sets the police on the trail of a mysterious scam involving cinema cashiers.

books.title: The Worst Journey in the World
books.author: Apsley Cherry-Garrard
books.summary: The travelogue of a member of Robert Scott’s ill-fated Antarctic expedition of 1910.

books.title: The Genealogy of Morals
books.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
books.summary: Three essays analyzing the origins, meaning, and value of the concepts of good, evil, and bad; of guilt, punishment, and bad conscience; and of ascetic ideals, including those of truth and truthfulness.

books.title: Poetry
books.author: James Weldon Johnson
books.summary: A collection of poems by James Weldon Johnson.

books.title: A Woman of No Importance
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.summary: A mother’s secretive past clashes with high society when her son is offered an irresistible position with a charismatic nobleman.

books.title: The Big Time
books.author: Fritz Leiber
books.summary: A motley collection of servicemembers escape a plot as two mysterious factions wage an epic war across time itself.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Frederik Pohl
books.summary: A collection of science fiction stories by Frederik Pohl ordered by date of publication.

books.title: The Imitation of Christ
books.author: Thomas à Kempis
books.summary: Thomas à Kempis provides instructions for a Christian spiritual life.

books.title: Essays
books.author: Henry David Thoreau
books.summary: A collection of Henry David Thoreau’s essays.
